commit.message:
Fix: prevent "(default)" label from being used as model ID

When the server sends model:"(default)" in session_ready, it was stored
as selectedModel and later passed to the SDK on reconnect, causing
"Model (default) is not available" errors.

commit.message:
Try fixing the loadtests with connection reuse for UWSGI services (#81)

# Changes


## Affected services
- bridge
- city_pass
- construction_work
- contact
- core
- image
- modules
- notification
- survey
- waste

## Definition of done
- [ ] API is backwards compatible (`make openapi-diff`)
- [ ] Swagger UI up-to-date & tested (`make dev`)
- [ ] Dependencies updated (`make requirements`)
- [ ] Infrastructure config updated (aapp_azure_infra)
- [ ] Loadtests for relevant endpoints (aapp_testing_loadtests)

After PR created (and deployed on dev):
- [ ] Manual check on dev (o) e.g. use feature via Swagger or admin
panel
- [ ] Sanity check on test app (check that dev environment is selected)

## Other notes

GitHub Copilot was used in writing the code

---------

Remove sample data and legacy code; browse uses real DB books

- Remove sample book seeding from BookStore (init method deleted)
- Delete BookController.java (static 6 hardcoded browse cards)
- Delete BookListing.java (only used by BookController)
- Delete BookDetail.java and BookDetailStore.java (pre-refactor legacy)
- BookCollectionController now returns empty list (static data removed)
- App.jsx: browse now fetches from /api/book-details (real DB books)
- BookCard updated to use Book entity (cover from first edition, series badge)
- Navigation now uses book ID instead of title
- BookDetailPage updated to fetch by ID (/api/book-details/{bookId})
- Cleared sample books from PostgreSQL DB

commit.message:
feat: unified cycle-truth contract, specialization admission redesign, partitioned knowledge memory (#282)

* feat: unified cycle-truth contract, specialization admission redesign, partitioned knowledge memory

Task 1 — Cycle Truth Contract (pipeline_progress + cycle_analytics):
- Add CYCLE_TRUTH_TERMINAL_BLOCK_REASON enum (DISPATCH_BLOCKED, GOVERNANCE_BLOCKED,
  NO_PLANS_GENERATED, PLAN_NOT_APPROVED, UNSPECIFIED_EARLY_EXIT) to cycle_analytics.ts
- Compute cycleTruthContract in computeCycleAnalytics: isFullyCovered=true when all
  events are present OR a terminal block reason is explicitly classified
- Add cycleTruthContract to CYCLE_ANALYTICS_SCHEMA.cycleRecord.required
- Persist terminalBlockReason in pipeline_progress on cycle_complete with
  whitespace-trim guard; document in PIPELINE_PROGRESS_SCHEMA.conditionalFields

Task 2 — Specialization Admission Redesign (capability_pool + capacity_scoreboard):
- Add INFEASIBLE_TOPOLOGY to SPECIALIST_REROUTE_REASON_CODE (weight=0.00)
- Add computeTopologyFeasibility() to distinguish structural absence from under-utilization
- Update evaluateSpecializationAdmissionGate with optional 5th param topologyFeasibility;
  returns bypassType field: 'infeasible_topology' | 'bounded_fallback' | 'true_under_utilization'
- Add infeasibleTopologyBypassCount + trueUnderutilizationCount JSDoc to CapacityEntry

Task 3 — Partitioned Knowledge Memory (self_improvement + trust_boundary + jesus_supervisor):
- Add KNOWLEDGE_MEMORY_SCHEMA_VERSION=2, MEMORY_PARTITION enum
- Add migrateKnowledgeMemoryV1ToV2(): recent 20 lessons -> working, older -> episodic
- Auto-migrate on loadKnowledgeMemory; never write v1 back
- Add getKnowledgeMemoryAllLessons(), pushKnowledgeMemoryLesson(), setKnowledgeMemoryPromptHints()
- Add retrieveMemoryForContext() for fused trust-weighted retrieval across partitions
- Add rerankMemoryByTrust() to trust_boundary for trust-sorted topK results
- Update jesus_supervisor to union working+episodic lessons (v1/v2 backward compat)
- Migrate state/knowledge_memory.json to v2 (schemaVersion:2, 20 working, 95 episodic)

Fix: update BLOCK_REASON canonical list in orchestrator_pipeline_progress.test.ts
to include PENDING_CI_REPAIR added by another worker

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: revert CANONICAL_BLOCK_REASONS change - CI uses base branch orchestrator.ts without PENDING_CI_REPAIR

The previous commit mistakenly added PENDING_CI_REPAIR to the canonical list because
the local working tree had another worker's uncommitted modification to orchestrator.ts.
The CI runs against the base branch which does not have PENDING_CI_REPAIR, so the
test should NOT include it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 6 Step 6: make accessor helpers Value::Property-aware

Update get_getter, get_setter, has_getter, has_setter, lookup_getter, and
lookup_setter to detect inline accessors in Value::Property before falling
back to legacy __get_<key>/__set_<key> hidden-key lookups.

This is safe because the GetProperty handler now handles Value::Property
with an early return (Step 3), so the legacy accessor path never executes
for Value::Property values.  Other callers either short-circuit on
contains_key(key) or are in contexts (private fields) that don't use
Value::Property.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(tui): stabilize loading and list keys

Tighten the TUI interactions so loading states stay visually stable
and list navigation behaves consistently across screens.

This keeps search typing isolated from playback shortcuts, adds
page-style H/L movement in list screens, preserves series access on
lowercase s, and aligns the loading behavior in home, library, and
series with placeholder rows instead of loading-text flashes.

It also updates the README to match the current keybindings and Go
support, and caches empty library results so revisiting empty
libraries does not reintroduce unnecessary loading churn.

commit.message:
Distinguish TooManySponsoring from LowReserve in trust flag revoke

When AllowTrust/SetTrustLineFlags revokes authorization and redeems
pool-share trustlines into claimable balances, sponsorship sandwich
creation could fail because num_sponsoring is at u32::MAX. Previously
this was unconditionally mapped to LowReserve; now it correctly
propagates as OpTooManySponsoring, matching stellar-core.

Also replaces panic on non-sandwich sponsorship exhaustion with a
proper TooManySponsoring error return.

Fixes #1198

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix off-by-one in offer crossing limit

Move maxOffersToCross check to BEFORE cross_offer_v10(), matching
stellar-core which checks offerTrail.size() >= maxOffersToCross before
crossing. Previously the check was after crossing, causing the exact
1000th crossing to be rejected with opEXCEEDED_WORK_LIMIT when
stellar-core allows it.

Fixes #1212

fix: eliminate Rollup UNRESOLVED_IMPORT build warnings (#97)

## Summary

- **Rename bare `'crypto'` → `'node:crypto'`** in 5 server routes and
`scripts/generate-secret.ts`. The `svelte-adapter-bun` rolldown pass
externalizes `/^node:/` but not bare specifiers like `'crypto'`. In Bun,
`node:crypto` resolves to Bun's native implementation (faster than the
Web Crypto global `crypto.randomUUID()` which goes through
JavaScriptCore).

- **Add `@sentry/sveltekit` to `ssr.external`** in `vite.config.ts`.
SvelteKit 2.21.3+ started bundling Sentry during SSR builds (see
[sveltejs/kit#13888](https://github.com/sveltejs/kit/issues/13888)),
which drags in OpenTelemetry's bare `async_hooks` dynamic import into
the adapter's rolldown input. Externalizing Sentry prevents this; the
adapter's rolldown then handles it via the `dependencies` regex (Sentry
is already in `dependencies`). This is Sentry's own documented
workaround.

## Residual warning

One warning intentionally remains:

```
.svelte-kit/adapter-bun/chunks/db.sqlite.js (2:25) [UNRESOLVED_IMPORT]
  Could not resolve 'bun:sqlite'
```

Our `src/lib/server/db.sqlite.ts` correctly imports `bun:sqlite` (a Bun
built-in resolved at runtime). The adapter's rolldown externals list has
no regex matching `bun:*` and offers no option to extend it. A fix
requires an upstream PR to `svelte-adapter-bun`. The code works
correctly at runtime.

## Related

Companion to dokterbob/mutuvia#96 (deps/devDeps cleanup). The
`drizzle-orm` internal driver warnings were eliminated by reverting
`drizzle-orm` to `dependencies` in that PR.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run check` — pass
- [x] `bun run test` — pass
- [x] `bunx playwright test` — pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: dokterbob <22742+dokterbob@users.noreply.github.com>

legacy hidden-key markers alongside Value::Property are redundant:

- runner.rs: remove write_attrs_to_legacy_map for strict arguments 'callee'

This eliminates 5 hidden-key entries per fn_proto / arguments object.

Implement text sidecar + manifest pipeline for source document processing

- Add core text extraction pipeline for PDFs and images
  - sourceHashing: SHA-256 hashing for files, S3 objects, buffers
  - sourceExtractor: PDF text extraction with Tesseract OCR fallback
  - sidecarWriter: Atomic S3 sidecar writing (temp+rename pattern)
  - manifestService: JSONL manifest operations (load/upsert/save)
  - sourcePipeline: Batch processFolder and verifyFolder orchestration

- Add supporting utilities and tests
  - portablePaths: Cross-platform path handling
  - sourceExtraction.test.ts: Unit tests for hashing, manifest, paths

- Add CLI tool for manual processing and verification
  - scripts/process-sources.ts: process, verify, help commands

- Integrate with server
  - Hook into /api/source-upload endpoint
  - Async processing via setImmediate (non-blocking)

- Add comprehensive documentation
  - SIDECAR_IMPLEMENTATION.md: Complete technical documentation
  - IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md: High-level overview
  - SIDECAR_QUICK_REFERENCE.md: Quick reference guide

- Add OCR dependency
  - Install tesseract.js (^7.0.0) for image text extraction

Features:
  ✓ Automatic processing on source upload
  ✓ PDF text extraction with OCR fallback
  ✓ Image OCR for .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .tif, .tiff, .webp
  ✓ Atomic sidecar writing to S3
  ✓ JSONL manifest with SHA-256 integrity checks
  ✓ Manifest verification (missing, stale, mismatched detection)

repository.name: Plothole
repository.description: Plothole is an AI-powered story analysis tool that reads your manuscript and automatically constructs a living encyclopedia of your narrative universe. Characters, locations, artifacts, themes, and timeline events—all extracted, organized, and cross-referenced in seconds.

commit.message:
fix: unify button action format to A2UI ActionSchema (#78)

* fix: unify button action format to A2UI ActionSchema

The btn() helper in web API response-processor used a custom flat format
(`action: "reply", data: { text }`) instead of the canonical A2UI
ActionSchema (`action: { event: { name, data } }`).

Updated btn() to emit the proper `{ event: { name: "reply", data } }`
shape, matching the ButtonAction interface in @kickstart/core/catalog
and the A2UI v0.9 ActionSchema from common-types.

All 30 B-25 contract tests pass. All 423 tests green.

Closes #24

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: append B-25 learning to Bender history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use context instead of data in btn() event payload

ActionSchema expects 'context' in the event object, not 'data'.
Using 'data' caused the replyText to be silently dropped on dispatch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: reconcile data/context wording to match A2UI v0.9 ActionSchema

Address Copilot review comments on PR #78:
- history.md: fix btn() shape description to reference event.context (not event.data)
- response-processor.ts: add inline comment clarifying A2UI v0.9 ActionSchema
  uses event.context vs @kickstart/core's ButtonAction event.data
- PR description updated to reference context instead of data

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: append review-fix learning to bender history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix stock quantity limit in shopping cart

- Prevent adding items to cart beyond available stock
- Check maxStock (car.stock_quantity) before incrementing quantity
- Only allow adding new items to cart if stock > 0
- Store stock_quantity with cart item for future reference
- Existing cart increment protection already in place

commit.message:
Phase 6 Step 4: make individual mark helpers Value::Property-aware

Update has_nonenumerable_mark, has_readonly_mark, and has_nonconfigurable_mark
to check inline PropAttrs from Value::Property before falling back to legacy
hidden-key scans.

has_readonly_mark has an accessor guard: Value::Property with getter/setter
always returns false (WRITABLE doesn't apply to accessor properties per spec).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
